evidence_urls: https://subtelforum.com/pakistan-internet-fully-restored-nationwide-by-monday/
original_text: Over the past few months, several regional submarine cables that carry much of the country’s traffic, including IMEWE and SEA-ME-WE4 have experienced outages.
title: https://subtelforum.com/pakistan-internet-fully-restored-nationwide-by-monday/
url: https://subtelforum.com/pakistan-internet-fully-restored-nationwide-by-monday/

affected_area: Pakistan (especially upper Punjab and federal capital)
evidence_urls: https://subtelforum.com/internet-services-restored-after-repair-of-submarine-cable-ptcl/
original_text: Pakistan Tele­commu­nications Limited (PTCL) on Wednesday said the international cable consortium had completed the repair of a repeater installed in the submarine cable, which had affected internet speeds across the country. Yesterday, internet users, already facing disruptions in parts of upper Punjab and the federal capital due to law and order situation, experienced slow connectivity. PTCL had said that a maintenance activity was planned on one of the company's submarine cables to repair a faulty repeater by the international cable consortium.
title: https://subtelforum.com/internet-services-restored-after-repair-of-submarine-cable-ptcl/
url: https://subtelforum.com/internet-services-restored-after-repair-of-submarine-cable-ptcl/

accident_location: Between Scarinish on Tiree and Calgary on Mull

evidence_urls: https://subtelforum.com/tiree-subsea-cable-repairs-begin-after-storm-damage/
normalized_cable_id: BT Highlands and Islands Submarine Cable System
original_text: Good news for residents and businesses on the tiny, remote Inner Hebrides Island of Tiree, which suffered a break in its only subsea fibre cable after Storm Amy struck Scotland at the start of October – resulting in disruption to local broadband and phone services (here). Cable repairs are finally about to get underway.
title: https://subtelforum.com/tiree-subsea-cable-repairs-begin-after-storm-damage/
url: https://subtelforum.com/tiree-subsea-cable-repairs-begin-after-storm-damage/

evidence_urls: https://subtelforum.com/kamchatka-faces-5-day-internet-outage-for-cable-repairs/
original_text: Authorities in Far East Russia’s Kamchatka Peninsula said Wednesday that urgent upgrades to an undersea cable would cause a regionwide internet outage for at least five days starting next week.The regional government said the state telecommunications provider Rostelecom would carry out “large-scale” work on the Sakhalin-Kamchatka fiber-optic line from Sept. 25 to 29, upgrading land-based infrastructure to prevent longer outages if the cable erodes or is damaged.
reason: urgent upgrades to prevent longer outages if the cable erodes or is damaged
title: https://subtelforum.com/kamchatka-faces-5-day-internet-outage-for-cable-repairs/
url: https://subtelforum.com/kamchatka-faces-5-day-internet-outage-for-cable-repairs/

original_text: Reliable sources have informed Nicobar Times that ASEAN Restorer has been mobilized to undertake repair work on the CANI cable fault in Car Nicobar-Kamorta Segment that has disrupted connectivity in Nancowry and Great Nicobar since 20th September 2025.

evidence_urls: https://subtelforum.com/internet-outage-in-pakistan-to-last-4-5-weeks/
original_text: The Ministry of Information Technology told a National Assembly panel that fixing the undersea internet cables cut near Yemen will take four to five weeks. Secretary IT Zarrar Hasham briefed the NA Standing Committee on Information Technology on Thursday. He said that four to five cables were damaged, not just one or two as first reported. The damage off the coast of Yemen has caused the most serious disruption.
title: https://subtelforum.com/internet-outage-in-pakistan-to-last-4-5-weeks/
url: https://subtelforum.com/internet-outage-in-pakistan-to-last-4-5-weeks/

cable_name: Undersea internet cables near Saudi Arabia
evidence_urls: https://subtelforum.com/internet-speed-in-pakistan-remains-slow/
original_text: The slowdown is due to a fault in undersea internet cables near Saudi Arabia, which has not yet been fixed, causing serious difficulties for consumers.PTCL confirmed that reduced internet speed is a result of the submarine cable fault, leading to partial service disruption nationwide. The impact is more noticeable during evening hours. However, efforts are being made to improve internet speed.PTCL has not given a specific timeline for full restoration but assured that the fault is being addressed as quickly as possible. Until repairs are complete, users will continue to face service issues.Officials added that repairing undersea cables requires specialized ships and favorable weather conditions, which means the process could take weeks.
title: https://subtelforum.com/internet-speed-in-pakistan-remains-slow/
url: https://subtelforum.com/internet-speed-in-pakistan-remains-slow/

title: https://subtelforum.com/submarine-cable-cuts-disrupt-internet-services-in-pakistan/
url: https://subtelforum.com/submarine-cable-cuts-disrupt-internet-services-in-pakistan/

evidence_urls: https://subtelforum.com/telkom-plans-papua-submarine-cable-repair/
original_text: Telkom to Repair Damaged Submarine Cable Disrupting Papua Networks August 18, 2025 Jayapura (ANTARA) – State-owned PT Telekomunikasi Indonesia (Telkom) is preparing a special vessel to repair a broken submarine cable that has disrupted networks in South and Central Papua. The first stage is temporary and scheduled for the first week of September. The second stage, which will be permanent, is planned for mid-September. Telkom, Indonesia's largest telecom provider, said broadband services had declined following an August 16 disruption.
title: https://subtelforum.com/telkom-plans-papua-submarine-cable-repair/
url: https://subtelforum.com/telkom-plans-papua-submarine-cable-repair/

accident_location: Approximately 5 nautical miles offshore from Taiwan
affected_area: Communications and data between Taiwan and Penghu
cable_name: Undersea communications cable (exact name not specified)
evidence_urls: https://subtelforum.com/taiwan-charges-chinese-captain-in-undersea-cable-case/
original_text: Prosecutors in Taiwan filed charges against a Chinese captain from the mainland alleging he deliberately instructed his crew to anchor above a key undersea communications cable. According to the Taiwan Tainan District Prosecutors Office, the captain was aware that he was in a restricted area and used the claw of his anchor to snag and damage the cable. Chunghwa Telecom reported an interruption of service on one of its cables for communications and data that connects Taiwan and Penghu.
reason: Deliberate anchoring above the cable by a Chinese captain
title: https://subtelforum.com/taiwan-charges-chinese-captain-in-undersea-cable-case/
url: https://subtelforum.com/taiwan-charges-chinese-captain-in-undersea-cable-case/

accident_location: S9 segment to Singapore and S1.9 segment to Malaysia
evidence_urls: https://subtelforum.com/vietnam-internet-restored-after-apg-fix/
original_text: Vietnam’s global internet connectivity has returned to normal following the completion of repairs to the Asia Pacific Gateway (APG) undersea cable—just in time for the country’s five-day Reunification Day and May Day holiday starting Wednesday. According to an internet service provider, the APG cable—responsible for connectivity issues since February—has been fully repaired. Fixes were completed on the S9 segment to Singapore and the S1.9 segment to Malaysia, significantly improving access speeds for Vietnamese users relying on international services.
title: https://subtelforum.com/vietnam-internet-restored-after-apg-fix/
url: https://subtelforum.com/vietnam-internet-restored-after-apg-fix/

original_text: Early this month, Taiwan’s coast guard said it had intercepted the Xing Shun 39 — a Hong Kong-owned freighter carrying the Cameroonian and Tanzanian flags — after Taiwan’s biggest telecom company, Chunghwa Telecom, alerted authorities that an international undersea cable had been damaged on Jan. 3. A “preliminary assessment” suggested the damage might have been caused by the freighter, which “transited the area at the time of the incident,” the coast guard said.
reason: Potential sabotage or accidental damage by the freighter Xing Shun 39

evidence_urls: Yemen Government Probes Delay AAE-1 Cable Repairs.txt
original_text: This week, reports suggest that the Yemeni government has contacted members of the AAE-1 (Asia-Africa-Europe 1) submarine cable consortium, informing them that they are being probed with regard to ties to the nation’s Houthi rebels....Crucially, while this investigation is ongoing, the official Yemeni government will not give permission for cable ships to repair AAE-1, which was damaged along with two other cables (EIG and SEACOM/TGN-Eurasia) back in February....One of these attacks caused a vessels anchor to be dragged across the trio of subsea cables, severing them and causing major telecommunications disruption across the Middle East, Europe, and Africa.
reason: Attack by Muslim Houthis causing a vessel's anchor to be dragged across the trio of subsea cables
title: Yemen Government Probes Delay AAE-1 Cable Repairs.txt
url: Yemen Government Probes Delay AAE-1 Cable Repairs.txt

evidence_urls: MainOne Completes Repairs to Restore Internet.txt
original_text: MainOne, a provider of undersea fiber optic internet connectivity in West Africa, has announced the successful repair of its submarine cable system. The issue with the cable which occurred on March 14, 2024, disrupted internet services across the region.
reason: Issue with the submarine cable system
title: MainOne Completes Repairs to Restore Internet.txt
url: MainOne Completes Repairs to Restore Internet.txt

evidence_urls: Pakistan Internet Issues Linked to Faulty Submarine Cable.txt
original_text: The PTA now says the nationwide slow internet is due to a “faulty submarine cable”. ...However, a major dip in internet services has allegedly been traced back to a major internet service provider (ISP) making a “severe misconfiguration in their core network”. ...Blame was attributed to various issues, including “cutting of one of the submarine cables (SMW4) for last one month or so”. This has apparently reduced the inland capacity by 1.5 terabits (Tbit) per second.
reason: Faulty submarine cable, cutting of the cable, severe misconfiguration in the core network of a major internet service provider
title: Pakistan Internet Issues Linked to Faulty Submarine Cable.txt
url: Pakistan Internet Issues Linked to Faulty Submarine Cable.txt

accident_location: Near the crossing with Le Trou Sans Fond Canyon, an underwater canyon off the coast of Abidjan
evidence_urls: Ghana’s Submarine Cables Repaired.txt
original_text: On March 14 this year, Ghana's data services were disrupted due to underwater cable damage. All four undersea cable landing service providers in Ghana (ACR, MainOne, SAT-3, and WACS) were disconnected from international data services, resulting in a complete internet shutdown. According to the subsea cable service providers, all four cable faults were discovered near the crossing with Le Trou Sans Fond Canyon, an underwater canyon off the coast of Abidjan. Dr. Anokye stated that the SAT-3 cable was restored on April 6 and the ACE cable on April 17, 2024. “The WACS cable was repaired on April 29, 2024, and the MainOne cable repairs were completed on May 8, 2024,” he added.
title: Ghana’s Submarine Cables Repaired.txt
url: Ghana’s Submarine Cables Repaired.txt

evidence_urls: Submarine Cable Fault Disrupts Internet in Pakistan.txt
original_text: Due to a fault in the global submarine cable, the internet service was affected in some parts of Pakistan. According to the Pakistan Telecommunication Authority (PTA), a malfunction has occurred in the international submarine cable SMW4, which is one of the seven cables connecting Pakistan to the globe through the internet web. Due to the malfunction, the users may experience slow internet service during peak hours.
reason: Fault in the global submarine cable
title: Submarine Cable Fault Disrupts Internet in Pakistan.txt
url: Submarine Cable Fault Disrupts Internet in Pakistan.txt

Based on preliminary findings the fault resulted from an incident on our submarine cable system, offshore the coast of West Africa, that has also affected other cable systems and disrupting international connectivity and internet services across West Africa.
title: MainOne Update on Undersea Cable Restoration.txt

affected_area: Traffic to the Middle East and Central and Eastern Europe
evidence_urls: Another Internet Cable Fault Hits South Africa.txt
original_text: Several links on the Eastern Africa Submarine System (EASSy) have been offline since Tuesday due to shunt fault, MyBroadband has learned. An industry source with knowledge of the situation explained that traffic to the Middle East and Central and Eastern Europe was the most affected and seeing increased latency. However, unlike the incidents earlier this year, the fault does not appear to have caused major disruptions to the continent’s Internet.
title: Another Internet Cable Fault Hits South Africa.txt
url: Another Internet Cable Fault Hits South Africa.txt

url: Massive Internet Outages Have Been Reported Across Africa.txt

accident_location: West African coast, offshore Cote D'Ivoire, in the Atlantic Ocean
evidence_urls: Submarine Cable Repairs May Last Five Weeks – MainOne.txt
original_text: MainOne, a leading provider of submarine cable systems, has revealed that the repair process for its damaged submarine cable may take up to five weeks. The disruption left numerous major Nigerian banks offline, causing inconvenience to customers who were unable to access banking apps or utilise USSD services. Preliminary investigations conducted by the company have attributed the fault to an external incident, resulting in a cut along the West African coast, offshore Cote D'Ivoire, in the Atlantic Ocean.
reason: Fault attributed to an external incident
title: Submarine Cable Repairs May Last Five Weeks – MainOne.txt
url: Submarine Cable Repairs May Last Five Weeks – MainOne.txt

affected_area: Nigeria, Ghana, Senegal, Cote d’Ivoire, and other African countries
evidence_urls: Seabed Vibrations Likely Behind MainOne Cable Cuts.txt
original_text: MainOne has disclosed that seismic activity on the seabed may have caused cable breaks impacting Internet access in Nigeria, Ghana, Senegal, Cote d’Ivoire, and other African countries. In a recent update, the digital infrastructure company, which has been one of the most hit, ruled out human activity as the cause of the cable cut, given the depth of the fault.
title: Seabed Vibrations Likely Behind MainOne Cable Cuts.txt
url: Seabed Vibrations Likely Behind MainOne Cable Cuts.txt

evidence_urls: Vava’u and Ha’apai Suffer Third Cable Blackout.txt
original_text: A total communications failure on Tonga's only inter-island domestic submarine cable has continued to severely impact telephone and internet services to the outer islands since 29 June, and it is likely to be several weeks before it is known how long it might take for normal services to be restored. Tonga Cable Acting CEO, Sosafate Kolo said the cause of the disruption to the cable is unknown. Tonga Cable believed that the cable damage was caused by an earthquake that occured early morning on Saturday, 29 June, as the cable failure occured after the earthquake.
reason: Multiple landslides resulting from an earthquake
title: Vava’u and Ha’apai Suffer Third Cable Blackout.txt
url: Vava’u and Ha’apai Suffer Third Cable Blackout.txt

original_text: We have determined that multiple fibre cables on the West Coast of Africa — WACS, MainOne, SAT3, ACE — have been impacted which reduced total capacity supporting our regions in South Africa.

url: South Africa Internet Hit by Multiple Subsea Cable Breaks.txt

accident_location: Off the coast of KwaZulu-Natal, South Africa
evidence_urls: 2024 East Africa Submarine Cable Outage Report.txt
original_text: On 12 May 2024, two fiber optic submarine cables—SEACOM and EASSy—were damaged off the coast of KwaZulu-Natal, South Africa, significantly reducing Internet connectivity in multiple East African countries. The cause of the damage is a suspected dragging ship anchor, a common source of submarine cable cuts. All cables were repaired as of 3 June 2024—approximately three weeks after the initial damage.
title: 2024 East Africa Submarine Cable Outage Report.txt
url: 2024 East Africa Submarine Cable Outage Report.txt

affected_area: Connections between Asia and Europe, and Yemen’s waters
evidence_urls: Finally, Progress With Red Sea Cable Repairs.txt
original_text: Repairs have finally commenced on three subsea telecommunications cables that were damaged in the Red Sea in February, even as Houthi militants escalate their attacks on ships in the area. The AAE-1 cable, a 25 000km fibre-optic link between Asia and Europe, was repaired by a ship owned by E-Marine, a subsidiary of Abu Dhabi-based Emirates Telecommunications Group. The cable came online this week, a Yemeni government official said. The same ship, Niwa, remains in Yemeni waters to repair the remaining two cables, Seacom and EIG. Repairs to the cables have depended on gaining access to infrastructure in Yemen’s waters, a task complicated by the country’s split government and the fact the Red Sea is a conflict zone.
reason: Severed by the anchor of a cargo ship sunk by Houthi militants
title: Finally, Progress With Red Sea Cable Repairs.txt
url: Finally, Progress With Red Sea Cable Repairs.txt

accident_location: Link between Kuwait and Al-Khobar, Saudi Arabia
evidence_urls: Kuwait Internet Restored After Submarine Cable Disruption.txt
original_text: The Communication and Information Technology Regulatory Authority (CITRA) of Kuwait recently reported a significant disruption affecting internet services in the country. The incident arose from a fault in the Falcon international submarine cable operated by Global Cloud Xchange (GCX), specifically impacting the link between Kuwait and Al-Khobar, Saudi Arabia. Notably, the disruption occurred in an area outside of Kuwaiti territorial waters, resulting in a decline in the quality of internet services across the nation.
reason: Fault in the Falcon international submarine cable
title: Kuwait Internet Restored After Submarine Cable Disruption.txt
url: Kuwait Internet Restored After Submarine Cable Disruption.txt

evidence_urls: Submarine Cable Faults Disrupts Internet in Pakistan.txt
original_text: Pakistan’s telecom authority reiterated on Wednesday that faults in two international undersea Internet cables are to blame for slow Internet across the country, saying that one of them is likely to be repaired by early October. “The ongoing Internet slowdown across the country is mainly due to fault in two (SMW4, AAE-1) of the seven international submarine cables connecting Pakistan internationally,” the Pakistan Telecommunication Authority (PTA) said in a message. “It is updated that fault in SMW-4 submarine cable is likely to be repaired by early October 2024,” the authority added.
title: Submarine Cable Faults Disrupts Internet in Pakistan.txt
url: Submarine Cable Faults Disrupts Internet in Pakistan.txt

evidence_urls: Submarine Cable Break Slows Internet in Bangladesh.txt
original_text: Bangladeshi internet users are experiencing disruptions after the country's second submarine cable, SEA-ME-WE 5, the largest supplier of international bandwidth to Bangladesh, broke Friday night. The issue was identified at midnight. It will take at least two to three days to resume the connection. The undersea cable of the SEA-ME-WE 5 got broken in a spot between Singapore and Malaysia. So, a ship will be mobilised to repair and restore the service. The total operation will take minimum of two to three days.
title: Submarine Cable Break Slows Internet in Bangladesh.txt
url: Submarine Cable Break Slows Internet in Bangladesh.txt
